name: Cross-Modal Query Engine
overview: TrustDB's defining differentiator: queries that span KV lookups, graph traversals, spatial filters, and full-text search execute in a single engine with zero serialization boundaries. A full_well_onboarding query spanning all 4 modalities completes in 182µs — 82-330x faster than equivalent queries on a PostGIS + Neo4j + Elasticsearch stack that requires 3 network hops and 3 serialization round-trips.

howItWorks.title: Composable Primitives
howItWorks.description: The Lens API provides composable query building blocks: confidence-filtered, temporal, graph traversal, spatial, and search. These are Rust functions, not a string-based query language.

howItWorks.title: In-Process Execution
howItWorks.description: All modalities share the same B-epsilon tree storage. A spatial query feeds directly into a graph traversal which feeds into a search — all in the same process, same memory space, zero serialization.

howItWorks.title: Cascadeable Operations
howItWorks.description: Results from one modality automatically carry their confidence envelopes into the next. A spatial filter that narrows to 10 points passes those points (with confidence) directly to a graph traversal.

howItWorks.title: Zero Handoff Cost
howItWorks.description: Traditional stacks pay 5-20ms per system boundary (network + serialize + deserialize + query plan). TrustDB pays only the CPU cost of the operation itself.

comparisons.narrative: The fundamental insight: cross-modal query latency is dominated by system boundaries (network + serialization), not computation. By unifying all modalities in one engine, TrustDB eliminates the dominant cost factor.

competitors.approach: Three separate databases connected by application code
competitors.limitation: 15-60ms per cross-modal query due to 3 network hops and serialization

competitors.approach: Multi-model database with SQL-like query language
competitors.limitation: No confidence semantics, no governance, newer/less proven
No spatial, no confidence, traditional RBAC not cryptographic
Cloud-only, no embedded mode, no spatial, no confidence
